description: The culinary capital of Latin America — home to Central, Maido, and Astrid y Gastón, a city where Nikkei fusion was invented, where ceviche is religion, and where a city that looks grey from the plane reveals vibrant life at street level.

neighborhoodRecs: Miraflores — Tourist hub, cliff top views over the Pacific, safe and walkable, Kennedy Park, excellent restaurants.
Barranco — Lima's bohemian neighbourhood, street art, craft beer bars, beachside cliffs, art galleries, best nightlife.
San Isidro — Business and upscale residential, safest area, Central and top restaurants, quieter than Miraflores.
Surco / Chorrillos — Local neighbourhoods with authentic cheap food, beaches, away from the tourist bubble.
areasToAvoid: Centro Histórico and La Victoria after dark — petty crime and pickpocketing are common at night.
Public transport (combis and buses) — confusing, crowded, and notorious for theft. Use Uber or taxis.
Callao near the port — genuinely dangerous area, no tourist reason to visit.
scamWarnings: Taxi overcharging: always use Uber, InDriver, or Cabify — Lima has no reliable metered taxis and street cabs charge tourist prices.
Money changers on the street may give counterfeit bills — exchange currency at your hotel or a bank.
Restaurant 'tourist menu' traps near the central plaza — walk 2 streets in any direction for half the price.
Fake police: plain-clothes 'tourist police' who want to check your wallet for counterfeit bills. Real police wear uniforms.

airportTransfer.cheapest: Bus (Ejecutivo) from LIM airport to Miraflores — S/2.50, takes 45–90 min depending on traffic. Very unreliable with luggage.
airportTransfer.fastest: Uber from LIM — S/25–45 ($7–12), takes 30–45 min to Miraflores.
airportTransfer.recommended: Uber or InDriver app — by far the most convenient and safe. Book inside the arrivals hall using the app.
airportTransfer.taxiEstimate: Official taxi desk inside airport: S/60–80 to Miraflores. Overpriced but guaranteed safe.

tippingEtiquette: 10% tip at restaurants is expected. Check if it's been added as 'servicio' before adding more.
Tip hotel porters S/3–5 per bag.
Lima is the culinary capital of the Americas — treat fine dining seriously and book ahead (Central needs 2–3 months notice).
Never drink tap water in Lima. Bottled water or filtered water at restaurants only.
Humility and warmth go far — Limeños are proud of their city and appreciate genuine curiosity about Peruvian culture.

dailyCostBreakdown.accommodation: Hostel dorm S/30–60 | Budget hotel S/100–180 | Mid-range hotel S/250–500 | Boutique S/600–1,200
dailyCostBreakdown.meals: Ceviche at market S/15–25 | Lunch menu corriente S/12–18 | Restaurant dinner S/50–120 | Central tasting menu S/800+
dailyCostBreakdown.transport: Uber within city S/10–25 | Metropolitano bus S/2.50 | CombiRed bus S/1.50
dailyCostBreakdown.activities: Larco Museum S/30 | Pachacámac ruins S/15 | Paragliding at Miraflores cliffs S/80–150 | Cooking class S/150–250

packingTips: Light layers — Lima's garúa (coastal fog) means grey, cool days June–November even without rain.
Sunscreen — the sun breaks through between October and April with intensity at Lima's latitude.
Cash in soles — smaller restaurants and markets are cash-only.
A good book — Lima is a fantastic city to slow down in for a few days before or after Machu Picchu.
premium.bookingStrategy: December–April is the sunny season (5–8°C warmer). June–November is the grey season (garúa fog) but prices are lower. Central, Maido and Astrid y Gastón should be booked 4–8 weeks ahead.
localTips: Ceviche for lunch, not dinner — in Lima, ceviche is traditionally a lunch dish and the best cevicherías close at 5pm.
Mercado Surquillo #1 for the most authentic market — fish, chilis, purple corn, lucuma, cherimoya in one chaotic hall.
Paragliding from Miraflores cliffs over the Pacific costs S/80–150 and needs no experience — a spectacular way to see the city.
Barranco's walking bridge (Puente de los Suspiros) at dusk with a pisco sour from a nearby bar is a Lima classic.
The Larco Museum at night (open until 10pm) has an excellent in-house restaurant and far fewer visitors than during the day.
premium.bestTimeToVisit: December–March: warm, sunny, Lima at its most pleasant. The garúa season (June–November) is grey but excellent for food-focused trips.
dayTrips: Pachacámac — 40 min south, remarkable pre-Inca and Inca ceremonial complex overlooking the Pacific, often overlooked by tourists.
Chincha — 3.5 hours south, Afro-Peruvian culture, the heartland of Perú Negr music and dance tradition.
Paracas — 4 hours south, Ballestas Islands boat tour (Galápagos of Peru), Paracas National Reserve desert landscapes.
visaInfo: Citizens of the US, UK, EU countries, Canada, and Australia: no visa required for tourist stays up to 183 days — passport on arrival is sufficient
On arrival at Jorge Chávez International Airport (LIM) you will receive a tourist card (TAM) — keep this document as it must be surrendered on departure
Peru does not require a return ticket strictly but immigration may ask — having an onward booking avoids any questions at LIM
Citizens of neighbouring countries (Bolivia, Brazil, Chile, Ecuador, Colombia) can enter with national identity cards
Peru does not have a tourist e-visa system — all entry is processed on arrival at the airport
currencyTips: Currency: Peruvian Sol (PEN / S/). Notes: 10, 20, 50, 100, 200 PEN
Lima's Miraflores and Barranco districts are relatively card-friendly; cash essential in local markets, street food areas, and public transport
Best ATMs: BCP (Banco de Crédito del Perú) and Interbank have reliable service with foreign Visa/Mastercard; Scotiabank has competitive rates for US cards
Counterfeit notes are a real problem in Peru — inspect S/50 and S/100 bills carefully; use ATMs rather than street changers
USD is widely accepted in Miraflores tourist areas at an approximate rate — check current Sol/USD rate and confirm you are getting the right change
premium.simCard: Claro Peru and Movistar have the best 4G coverage in Lima and across Peru's coastal areas. Tourist SIMs are available at Lima Airport (LIM) in the arrivals hall. A 30-day SIM with 15–30 GB costs S/ 40–80. Coverage drops significantly in the Andes and Amazon — Claro has slightly better coverage in highland regions. eSIM via Airalo is a convenient option.
